title: dalbergia velutina var. maingayi
intro: Dalbergia velutina var. maingayi, also known as the Philippine ironwood, is a rare and endangered tree species native to the Philippines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dalbergia velutina var. maingayi include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and burns. In herbal preparations, the plant's heartwood is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to have various health benefits when consumed.
